acceptance_criteria: The model has an explicit external-constraint channel.
It can generate a case where domestic slack remains but external limits begin to bind.
The results are explainable as a complement to, not a negation of, the current thesis.
Tests cover both low-dependence and high-dependence cases.
constraints: Prefer a simple external bottleneck over a full international macro model.
Keep the mechanism interpretable to readers of the post.
Do not let the extension drift into general balance-of-payments modeling unless necessary.
description: Extend the current capacity or comparative models with an import-essential, FX-pass-through, or foreign-currency-pressure channel. The aim is to show that domestic monetary space does not erase real external limits, especially when a country depends heavily on imported essentials or foreign funding.
goal: Add a minimal model surface that shows how external dependence can bind before domestic bookkeeping does, even for a sovereign issuer.
implementation_notes: Implemented in repo by Claude. Added external_constraint.py with import leakage and FX-stress channels, generated external_constraint.png, and updated README and experiment entrypoints.
Planner verification on 2026-04-25: targeted sovereign_securities and external_constraint test modules passed cleanly.
title: External-constraint and imported-essentials extension
